ops.title: Live acceptance run for codex async plane (matrix items 3–5) in a fresh session
ops.description:
Needs a session started AFTER the codex plugin updated to 1.0.1 (done 2026-07-20; the channel MCP + agent plane mount at session start, so the shipping session could not run these). codex quota is back — full fidelity available.

Item 3 — THE acceptance test (the #78782 deadlock inverse): spawn a codex-wrapper-async owner (or any subagent with mcp__plugin_codex_codex-ask-channel__await): cheap first tool call collects the greeting + agent id → `codex-ask --dispatch --owner <id> -s <lane>` → park on await → assert the completion directive arrives as the park's tool result naming the reply_file → owner reads the disk reply and reports it.
Item 4 — relay fallback: an owner that stops before completion; assert the parent gets the relay nudge, wakes the owner via SendMessage, and the primed owner collects + reads disk.
Item 5 — park cancel: TaskStop a parked await; assert clean cancellation (notifications/cancelled, ~15s) and no daemon wedge.

Context: cc-notes log 4e22cc22 (build log), SKILL.md "Async Dispatch" section, agents/codex-wrapper-async.md. Items 1/2/6 already proven live 2026-07-20 (instant dispatch + registry; Monitor + --watch chat ping with a real pong reply; daemon lazy-autostart at the daemonrole path). The wake enqueue+drain is hermetically proven (TestOwnerWakeDirectiveLands, real daemon + real worker); item 3 is the in-harness proof.

ops.text: An owner subagent parked on the await tool is woken by a real codex completion directive naming its reply_file and reports the disk reply verbatim (item 3)
The relay fallback delivers to an owner that stopped before completion, via the parent's SendMessage wake (item 4)
TaskStop cancels a parked await cleanly with no daemon wedge (item 5)
